channel.title: Economy
channel.description: News, Analysis and Opinion from POLITICO

item.title: Canada’s prime minister says economic ties with US are a weakness that must be corrected
item.description: “We have to take care of ourselves because we can’t rely on one foreign partner,” Mark Carney said in a video address. “We can’t control the disruption coming from our neighbors."

media:content.media:title: Canada's Prime Minister Mark Carney, center, arrives to meet with Chinese President Xi Jinping, at the Great Hall of the People in Beijing, China, Friday, Jan. 16, 2026. (AP Photo/Vincent Thian, Pool)

item.dc:creator: By Associated Press

item.title: Biden enters the Always Be Closing phase of his first term
item.description: Suddenly, overnight, real progress has been teed up for the White House. Can the president deliver?

media:content.media:title: Those who have worked with Biden in the White House and on Capitol Hill say that the former seven-term senator relishes the role of legislative closer.

item.dc:creator: By Adam Cancryn, Jonathan Lemire and Christopher Cadelago
item.dc:contributor: By Adam Cancryn, Jonathan Lemire and Christopher Cadelago

item.title: China's fragility feeds the doom-mongers in Davos
item.description: Fêted at the World Economic Forum in 2017, Xi Jinping is now accused of torpedoing the global economy with his disastrous Zero Covid strategy.

media:content.media:title: The Economist Intelligence Unit estimates China's recent lockdown stands to chomp an annualized aggregate of 6 percent off Shanghai’s economic output.

item.dc:creator: By Stuart Lau and Phelim Kine
item.dc:contributor: By Stuart Lau and Phelim Kine

item.title: Dems' latest challenge: Draining the Hill's swamp (at least a little)
item.description: They have a flurry of bills to reconcile, splits among their own and a potential GOP filibuster to contend with. But voters might reward them.

media:content.media:title: Rep. Abigail Spanberger arrives to speak on Thursday in Culpeper, Va. Spanberger is one of a growing number of Democrats seeking to capitalize on public fury over stock trades.

item.dc:creator: By Nicholas Wu, Burgess Everett and Sarah Ferris
item.dc:contributor: By Nicholas Wu, Burgess Everett and Sarah Ferris

item.title: Biden’s war on inflation is a battle to change human behavior
item.description: The president needs people to overcome a new set of fears and direct their purchases into the areas of the service economy hit hardest by the coronavirus pandemic.

media:content.media:title: While consumer spending has bounced back, demand for goods has outpaced demand for services, according to Commerce Department data. The White House says the price increases are temporary, and that President Biden has moved to open transport bottlenecks.

item.dc:creator: By Lorraine Woellert

item.title: Biden's ‘I feel your pain’ conundrum
item.description: It’s tripped up the last two Democratic presidents and could trip up Biden too: How to sell a recovery when most voters aren’t feeling it.

media:content.media:title: President Joe Biden speaks about the recently passed $1.2 trillion Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act at the Port of Baltimore on Wednesday in Baltimore, Md.

item.dc:creator: By Christopher Cadelago and Eugene Daniels
item.dc:contributor: By Christopher Cadelago and Eugene Daniels
